skill.name: business-analyst
skill.description: Business analysis guidance for KPI design, dashboards, forecasting, customer analytics, market analysis, and data storytelling.
skill.summary: Plan business intelligence work and turn data questions into structured analysis and recommendations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: AI review found no executable code, network calls, credential access, or prompt injection in the single SKILL.md file. The static findings are false positives caused by business-analysis wording, a local resource reference, and the TAM/SAM/SOM market sizing acronym.

low_findings.title: False positive: local resource reference is not shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 26 as Ruby or shell backtick execution. The line only tells the assistant to open resources/implementation-playbook.md for examples; it does not execute a command or pass user input to a shell.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a Markdown inline path reference, not code. No shell operator, script, or executable instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: weak cryptography terms are business prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged multiple SKILL.md lines as weak cryptographic algorithms. The cited lines describe analytics, dashboards, KPI design, testing, data warehousing, visualization, and analysis methods, not MD5, DES, RC4, SHA-1, or crypto library usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is natural-language guidance and contains no cryptographic implementation. The flagged locations are ordinary business-analysis phrases.

low_findings.title: False positive: SAM acronym is market sizing terminology
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 178 as a Windows SAM database reference. The line uses TAM/SAM/SOM as market-sizing terminology, with no reference to Windows credential stores or local system files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding phrase explicitly discusses market sizing. There is no filesystem path, credential database access, or Windows-specific context.

low_findings.title: False positive: campaign analysis is not network reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged SKILL.md line 133 as network reconnaissance. The line describes marketing attribution and campaign effectiveness, not scanning hosts, ports, services, or networks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is a marketing analytics capability. No network tooling, IP ranges, host discovery, or scan instruction appears at this location.

content.value_statement: Teams often struggle to turn raw business questions into clear metrics, dashboards, and decisions. This skill structures analysis work across KPIs, forecasting, customer behavior, market sizing, and executive recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Frames business objectives, success criteria, constraints, and required inputs.
Designs KPI frameworks, OKRs, scorecards, metric hierarchies, and monitoring plans.
Plans dashboard requirements for Tableau, Power BI, Looker, Qlik Sense, and similar BI tools.
Guides customer, market, revenue, retention, cohort, and funnel analysis workflows.
Recommends statistical methods, forecasting approaches, and experiment designs for business questions.
Converts findings into executive narratives, visual storylines, and implementation guidance.
limitations: It does not access live company systems unless the user provides data or context.
It cannot verify source data quality without samples, definitions, and business rules.
It provides business guidance, not certified financial, legal, privacy, or compliance advice.
It references a playbook file that is not present in the scanned package.

use_cases.title: Define executive KPI reporting
use_cases.description: Create a metric framework, dashboard scope, and review cadence for leadership reporting.

use_cases.title: Investigate customer churn
use_cases.description: Structure churn analysis, cohort review, risk segmentation, and retention recommendations.

use_cases.title: Estimate a new market opportunity
use_cases.description: Plan TAM, SAM, SOM analysis with assumptions, data sources, scenarios, and decision criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Clarify an analysis request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me turn this business question into an analysis plan: [question]. Ask for missing inputs, define success criteria, and list the first analyses to run.

prompt_templates.title: Design a KPI framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a KPI framework for [business area]. Include North Star metric options, supporting metrics, definitions, owners, data sources, and review cadence.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a dashboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft dashboard requirements for [audience] tracking [business process]. Include views, filters, alerts, drilldowns, metric definitions, and adoption risks.

prompt_templates.title: Build an executive recommendation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this situation: [context and data summary]. Identify drivers, test assumptions, compare scenarios, and produce an executive recommendation with risks and next steps.

output_examples.input: We need better visibility into sales funnel performance.
output: A funnel metric map from lead source through closed revenue.
Recommended dashboard sections for volume, conversion, velocity, and leakage.
Data quality checks for lifecycle stage dates, source attribution, and duplicate accounts.
Action recommendations for the largest conversion gaps.

output_examples.input: Leadership wants a churn prediction plan.
output: A churn definition and cohort structure aligned to the business model.
Candidate predictors such as usage decline, support history, plan changes, and payment events.
A validation plan using holdout periods, lift charts, and retention outcome tracking.
Operational next steps for risk scoring and customer success outreach.

output_examples.input: We are considering a new product segment.
output: A market sizing structure using TAM, SAM, and SOM assumptions.
Primary and secondary data sources to validate demand and willingness to pay.
Scenario ranges for conservative, base, and aggressive adoption cases.
Decision criteria for product investment and go-to-market sequencing.

best_practices: Provide the business decision, audience, metric definitions, and available data before asking for analysis.
Ask the skill to separate assumptions, evidence, risks, and recommendations in every deliverable.
Validate outputs with business owners, source system experts, and data quality checks before acting.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a dashboard before defining the decision it must support.
Do not treat generated forecasts as reliable without historical validation and sensitivity analysis.
Do not use strategic recommendations without reviewing privacy, compliance, and financial constraints.

faq.question: Can this skill create dashboards directly?
faq.answer: It can design dashboard requirements, metrics, layouts, and workflows. It does not build dashboards inside BI tools by itself.

faq.question: Does it need raw data to be useful?
faq.answer: Raw data helps, but it can also create analysis plans, metric definitions, and stakeholder questions from business context.

faq.question: Can it support financial analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It can structure revenue models, unit economics, ROI analysis, scenarios, and sensitivity reviews.

faq.question: Can it help with customer analytics?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers churn, segmentation, cohorts, funnel performance, customer journeys, and voice of customer analysis.

faq.question: Is this skill safe for sensitive data?
faq.answer: The scanned skill contains no code that sends data anywhere. Users should still avoid sharing unnecessary confidential data.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
